enonce.texte: Match each word on the left with its correct definition on the right. Write the corresponding letter.

sousQuestions.reponse: e. The owner of a property who rents it to others
d. A person who pays rent to live in a property
a. Regular payment to use a property
b. A place to live, such as a house or apartment
c. An overcrowded, poor urban area with bad housing conditions

enonce.texte: Read each description and choose the correct housing word from the box: house, apartment, slum, residential area, real estate agent.

questions.texte: Describe a separate building for one family, often with a small garden.
A set of rooms for living on one floor of a tall building.
A professional who helps people find properties to buy or rent.
A district where houses are close together, away from factories or noise.
An area with very poor, informal housing lacking clean water or electricity.

intitule: Apply Housing Vocabulary to Real Life

enonce.texte:
Read the short situation. Then answer the questions using the vocabulary from Lesson 23.

Situation: Paul is a student in Yaoundé. He needs a place to live near his school. He has a small budget. He sees an advertisement: 'Studio available in Mokolo. Rent: 50,000 CFA per month. Water available, but sometimes no electricity. Landlord requires one month deposit.'

questions.texte: Identify the type of accommodation offered. Justify your answer.
questions.reponse: It is likely an apartment or a small studio (one-room accommodation). Justification: The word 'studio' indicates a small self-contained unit.

questions.texte: What problem might Paul face in this neighbourhood? Use a specific vocabulary word.
questions.reponse: He might face poor electricity supply (or unreliable utilities). This could be related to a 'slum' area if conditions are bad, but the text says 'sometimes no electricity'.

questions.texte: If Paul pays the deposit and the first month's rent, how much money does he give the landlord initially? Calculate.
Critique Paul's choice: Give one advantage and one disadvantage of renting this studio for a student.
questions.reponse: Advantage: It is near his school (from the text 'near his school' is implied) and affordable. Disadvantage: Unreliable electricity can make studying difficult at night.

enonce.texte: Look at the table below comparing two areas of Douala: Bonapriso (wealthy residential area) and Bepanda (mixed area with some slum conditions). Use comparative adjectives and housing vocabulary to answer.

questions.texte: Formulate one sentence comparing the rent in Bonapriso and Bepanda.
questions.reponse: The rent in Bonapriso is much higher than in Bepanda. (or: Rent in Bepanda is lower than in Bonapriso.)

questions.texte: Which area has better access to electricity? Justify using a superlative adjective.
questions.reponse: Bonapriso has the most reliable electricity. Justification: It has 24/7 electricity, while Bepanda has frequent outages.

questions.texte: Propose one solution to improve housing conditions in Bepanda's slum areas. Use the word 'landlord' or 'real estate agent'.
questions.reponse: Landlords should reinvest part of the rent to install clean water pipes and legal electricity connections. Or: Real estate agents could encourage investors to build affordable, decent housing.

questions.texte: Decide where a young family with a low income should live. Defend your choice using at least two vocabulary words from the lesson.
questions.reponse: They should live in Bepanda because the rent is affordable. Even though there are some slum conditions, they can save money. They should look for a small apartment with a reliable landlord.

intitule: Fill the Blanks: Real Estate Advertisement

enonce.texte: Complete the real estate advertisement with the correct words: located, accommodation, tenant, rent, residential area.

questions.texte: For ______: Quiet studio in a nice ______.
questions.reponse: For tenant: Quiet studio in a nice residential area.

questions.texte: Well-______ near the main market. Monthly ______: 40,000 CFA.
questions.reponse: Well-located near the main market. Monthly rent: 40,000 CFA.

questions.texte: This ______ is perfect for a single student.
questions.reponse: This accommodation is perfect for a single student.
